name: PAD THAI
description: In Thailand, pad Thai was a regular lunch for us and the perfect remedy for a Thai Bucket-induced hangover. It's a delicious, fresh-tasting dish that should satisfy everyone you cook it for, with spring onions around the bow. We serve it with some fresh tomato and chilli flakes, sweet chili sauce and sriracha!
ingredients: juice of 2 limes, plus extra lime wedges to serve
sweet chilli sauce, to serve, optional
method: Tofu press or 2 clean tea towels and a weight such as a heavy book, large, deep frying pan or a high heat | Large plate | Serving dish | Kitchen paper

Press the tofu using a tofu press or place it between two clean tea towels, lay it on a plate and put a weight on top. Leave for at least 1 hour to drain any liquid and firm up before you start cooking.
Put the rice noodles in a large bowl and pour over enough just-boiled water to cover them. Leave to soak for 10 minutes, then drain well.
Heat half the tofu and cut it into 1cm dice. Drain on kitchen paper for another time. Do serve the noodles.

Put the pan on a high heat and pour in 2 tablespoons of oil. Heat the pan and immediately reduce the heat to medium. Put in the pressed tofu, moving the tofu around gently browned. Transfer to a plate.
Put it finely, but in the same pan. Cover them with boil and simmer until they are tender, about until ready to flex the but tofu cooked.
Select the onion soften and briefly stir through. Then add soy instead cook until they've softened.
Peel and chop the onion and garlic. Rip the stem from the chilis and chop, removing the seeds if you prefer a milder heat. Trim and finely slice the spring onions. Set them all to one side.

Put the wok back on a high heat and add the remaining 2 tablespoons of oil. Add the garlic, ginger, chili and cook, stirring regularly, for 1-2 minutes. Add the spring onions for another 1-2 minutes. Add the noodles, breaking and loosening them gently so they don't stick together. Add the rice wine and stir for a further minute.

Return the tofu to the wok. With the beansprouts, fold lime juice and coriander, stirring any gaps in your other hand. Add the soy sauce. Stir in until the vegetables are slightly soft but still crunchy. Remove from the heat. Taste and add more soy sauce or chili sauce if needed.
Slice the green part of the spring onions into long, thin strips. Roughly chop the peanuts. Dot the remaining lime around the salad.
Divide the pad Thai between bowls with plenty of sliced red onions, peanuts and the spring onion ribbons. Serve with sweet chili sauce or sriracha on the side, if using.
